meta.title: Discover Spain | Premium Travel Guide
meta.description: A premium Spain travel guide — curated itineraries, food culture, and practical tips for the Iberian Peninsula.

culture.title: The Soul of Spain
culture.subtitle: Explore a land of passion — centuries of civilisation await

cards.title: Flamenco
cards.description: Born in Andalusia, blending Romani, Moorish and Spanish soul — a UNESCO Intangible Heritage since 2010.

cards.title: Gaudí Architecture
cards.description: Antoni Gaudí's visionary designs — Sagrada Família, Park Güell — forever changed the world's idea of architecture.

cards.title: Prado Museum
cards.description: One of the world's greatest art museums, with over 8,000 masterworks by Velázquez, Goya, and Rubens.

cards.title: Tapas Culture
cards.description: Spain's unique culinary tradition — small bites with great wine, the perfect way to socialise and savour life.

cards.title: Alhambra
cards.description: The Moorish palace masterpiece, with breathtaking arabesque decoration and serene water gardens.

cards.title: Gran Vía
cards.description: Madrid's Broadway — spectacular early-20th-century architecture, theatres and boutique shopping. The Metropolis Building is its iconic centrepiece.

cards.title: Barcelona Skyline
cards.description: The Mediterranean city at golden hour — the Columbus Monument and Barceloneta beach bathed in sunset light, with the Agbar Tower on the horizon.

cards.title: Mediterranean Coast
cards.description: The crystalline coves of Ibiza and Mallorca — limestone cliffs plunging into turquoise waters, the very essence of Spain's Mediterranean beauty.

visa.title: Essential Travel Guide
visa.visaSubtitle: Processing time ~15 working days · Visa fee ~€80
visa.warningText: Apply at least 2 months in advance. Allow extra time during peak season. Submit via the Spanish Embassy or an authorised visa centre.

checklist.detail: Valid for 6+ months beyond your stay, with at least 2 blank pages.
Completed truthfully and signed in person.
3.5×4.5 cm, white background, taken within the last 6 months.
3–6 months of statements showing sufficient funds (min. €65/day).
Coverage of at least €30,000 for the entire trip.
Flight booking and hotel reservation confirmations.
Required for employees; business owners provide a business licence.
Required for visiting friends or business travel.

phrases.title: Useful Spanish Phrases
phrases.subtitle: Master these phrases for a smoother journey · Click to hear pronunciation

portals.title: Handpicked Routes
portals.subtitle: Two carefully crafted journeys showcasing different faces of Spain

food.heroTagline: From Tapas to Paella — savour the soul of the Iberian table
food.philosophySubtitle: For Spaniards, eating is life itself — not merely sustenance

pillars.title: Slow Food Culture
pillars.text: Lunch at 2–4 pm, dinner at 9–11 pm. Mealtimes are the most important ritual of the day — never rushed.

pillars.title: Social Dining
pillars.text: Food is the heart of every gathering. Tapas culture invites people to share, taste and connect in a relaxed atmosphere.

pillars.title: Mediterranean Essence
pillars.text: Olive oil, fresh produce, seasonal cooking. Simple ingredients transformed into unforgettable dishes by skilled hands.

food.dishesSubtitle: Each one a window into Spanish culinary culture

dishes.name: Tapas
dishes.desc: The soul of Spanish small-plate culture

dishes.name: Paella
dishes.desc: The national dish, born in Valencia

dishes.name: Basque Bites
dishes.desc: Elegant bar snacks from the Basque Country

food.regionsSubtitle: Each region has its own distinct flavours and signature dishes

regions.name: Catalonia
regions.desc: Catalan cuisine is celebrated for its creativity and precision. The legendary El Bulli restaurant led a global culinary revolution, and La Boqueria market showcases world-class ingredients. Pair everything with locally produced Cava sparkling wine.

regions.name: Andalusia
regions.desc: The birthplace of tapas culture, shaped by the fusion of Moorish and Christian traditions. In Seville's old-town bars, a glass of Fino sherry with perfectly plated Gazpacho and Jamón is the most authentic Andalusian experience.

regions.name: Valencia
regions.desc: The home of authentic Paella Valenciana, made with locally grown Bomba rice and fresh Mediterranean seafood. Horchata de Chufa is a uniquely Valencian summer drink, and golden citrus groves fill the region with sunshine and fragrance.

regions.name: Basque Country
regions.desc: The region with the highest density of Michelin stars in the world. San Sebastián has more starred restaurants per capita than any other city. Street-level Pintxos bars are the perfect starting point, paired with a crisp glass of Txakoli white wine.

food.tipsSubtitle: Eat like a local with these insider tips

tips.title: Meal Times
tips.body: Lunch: 13:00–16:00; Dinner: 21:00–23:00. Don't arrive for dinner before 20:00 — you may be the only one there.

tips.title: Tapas Etiquette
tips.body: Stand at the bar to order, and pair freely with beer or wine. In some regions, a free tapa comes with every drink ordered.

tips.title: Tipping Culture
tips.body: Not compulsory. A 5–10% tip is appreciated for good service. At casual tapas bars, leaving small coins is perfectly fine.

tips.title: Wine Pairing
tips.body: Rioja red with meat and cured ham; Albariño white with seafood; Cava sparkling wine with starters.

tips.title: Ordering Phrases
tips.body: "Una ración" = full portion; "Una tapa" = small portion; "La carta, por favor" = the menu, please.

tips.title: Market Experience
tips.body: Mercado de la Boqueria (Barcelona) and Mercado de San Miguel (Madrid) are the top food markets to visit.

footer.brandDesc: A premium Spain travel guide crafted for travellers who seek depth, beauty, and authentic experience on the Iberian Peninsula.
